scenarios.reason: Outgoing bubbles: CSS sets background:#007AFF and color:#fff. Incoming bubbles: background:#E5E5EA and color:#000. Both match spec exactly.

Send button initializes with class 'inactive' (background:#AEAEB2) and disabled=true. On input event, when value.trim().length>0 it switches to class 'active' (background:#007AFF) and disabled=false. Correctly toggles on empty vs non-empty.

send() reads input.value.trim(), calls addBubble('out', txt, ...) appending a right-aligned blue bubble, then sets input.value='' and resets height. Input is cleared and new outgoing bubble is appended.
After send(), setTimeout fires after 1000–2000ms and calls addBubble('in', r, false) with a random AUTO_REPLIES entry. Incoming gray bubble appears within the 1–3 second window required.

All 6 rubric elements present: (1) Status bar shows '9:41' with signal/wifi/battery SVG icons; (2) Nav header has '<' chevron + '2' back label + centered contact name with avatar + video/phone icon buttons on right; (3) Bubble tails implemented via CSS clip-path pseudo-elements — .out .bubble::after points right, .in .bubble::before points left; (4) Pill-shaped input with border-radius:20px and #F2F2F7 background; (5) Circular send button (28px border-radius:50%) with white upward arrow SVG; (6) Colors match spec — #007AFF outgoing, #E5E5EA incoming, #F2F2F7 backgrounds. Minor: nav back label shows '2' (a badge number) rather than a text label like 'Back', but chevron is present and the overall composition reads as iOS Messages.

Config block is delimited by '/* === CONFIG — fork here === */' and '/* === END CONFIG === */' comments at the top of the script. Contains CONTACT_NAME (string), INITIAL_MESSAGES (array of objects), and AUTO_REPLIES (array of strings). All values are simple literals. The block is findable in under 5 seconds and entirely separate from application logic.
